title: T5: Archive Skill cross-session digest layer
scope: packages/cli (Stop/SessionEnd hook digest writer) + packages/shared (schema source_sessions[]) + packages/server (fab_extract_knowledge + fabric-archive Skill Phase 0.0)
description: Fix the single-session vs cross-session-trigger impedance mismatch. Stop hook accumulates edits across sessions to fire Signal A; when archive Skill runs, it should have visibility into all sessions since last `knowledge_proposed` event, not just current. Three sub-components: (1) Digest writer: Stop hook (or new SessionEnd hook if Claude Code supports) writes `.fabric/.cache/session-digests/<session_id>.md` per session — top 10 user messages + edit_paths + 1-line task title (~5KB). (2) Schema change: `fab_extract_knowledge` input `source_session: string` → `source_sessions: string[]` with single-string back-compat shim; generated pending frontmatter `source_sessions: [a, b, c]`. (3) Archive SKILL Phase 0.0: before viability gate, read events.jsonl, find session_ids since last `knowledge_proposed` event, load their digests, build cross-session context for Phase 0.5 + Phase 1.

files.change: augment Stop hook to write `.fabric/.cache/session-digests/<session_id>.md` containing: top 10 user messages (from hook input), unique edit_paths list, 1-line title inferred from first user message
new helper: `writeDigest({session_id, user_messages, edit_paths})`; ~5KB cap; atomic write to .fabric/.cache/session-digests/<session_id>.md
change input schema field `source_session: string` to `source_sessions: string[]`; add back-compat shim transforming legacy single string into [string]
update frontmatter type: `source_sessions: string[]` (array form); migrate any prior `source_session` reads to array-first
accept source_sessions[] from input; write array form to pending frontmatter; apply back-compat shim for legacy single-string callers

add Phase 0.0 'Collect cross-session digests': before viability gate, read events.jsonl tail, identify session_ids since last knowledge_proposed event, load each digest from .fabric/.cache/session-digests/, concatenate into Phase 0.5/1 context
test: writeDigest produces expected file shape; size capped at 5KB
add check: source_sessions[] accepted by fab_extract_knowledge; single-string back-compat works; pending frontmatter contains array form

implementation: Implement session-digest-writer.cjs helper with atomic write (tmp file + rename) and 5KB size cap (truncate user_messages if needed)
Wire digest write into fabric-hint.cjs Stop hook end-of-run; gracefully no-op if hook input lacks user_messages field
Update extract-knowledge.ts Zod schema: source_session (string, optional) → source_sessions (string array, min 1) + preprocess shim
Update fab_extract_knowledge.ts handler to write array form to frontmatter; ensure pending YAML serializer emits array correctly
Update fabric-archive SKILL.md with Phase 0.0 block: bash snippet showing events.jsonl tail filter + digest concatenation; document graceful degradation if digest directory missing
Test single-session case (back-compat path) still works; test multi-session case produces array frontmatter

reference.pattern: events.jsonl knowledge_proposed event from rc.5; .fabric/.cache/ sidecar pattern from rc.6 edit-counter
reference.examples: Digest file shape: H1 task title + H2 'User messages' bullet list + H2 'Edits' file list

criteria: GIVEN a session with user messages and edits ends WHEN Stop hook fires THEN `.fabric/.cache/session-digests/<session_id>.md` exists with file size ≤5KB AND contains top-10 user messages + edit_paths list
GIVEN fab_extract_knowledge called with `source_sessions: ['s1', 's2']` THEN generated pending frontmatter contains `source_sessions: [s1, s2]` (array form)
GIVEN legacy caller passes `source_session: 's1'` (string) THEN schema shim transforms to `source_sessions: ['s1']` without error

GIVEN events.jsonl has 3 sessions since last knowledge_proposed AND all 3 digests exist WHEN fabric-archive Skill Phase 0.0 runs THEN Skill loads and concatenates all 3 digest contents into working context

rationale.chosen_approach: Per-session digest sidecar + multi-session pending frontmatter + Skill-level read — keeps each layer simple (writer doesn't know about archive; archive doesn't need raw events)
alternatives_considered: Inline full session transcripts into pending (too large)
Store digests in DB (no DB yet)
decision_factors: Stop hook already has session context
Skill aggregation at archive time is rare (not per-message)
rationale.tradeoffs: Digest format may evolve — accepted, format is owned by hook+Skill pair, no third-party consumer

manual_checks: Run 3 short sessions; verify 3 digest files appear
Invoke fabric-archive; verify Phase 0.0 loads all 3 digests into context
success_metrics: fab_extract_knowledge accepts both legacy and new input forms
unit: Zod schema shim transforms legacy string
Pending YAML serializer emits array form
integration: End-to-end: 2 sessions → 2 digests → archive Skill → pending entry with source_sessions: [s1, s2]
